title: About Me
text:
My journey into the molecular world began at the University of Shiraz, where I completed my Bachelor’s degree in Biology. During my time there, I developed a deep fascination with the fundamental mechanisms of life, but I also recognized a growing gap: the sheer volume of biological data was outpacing our traditional methods of analysis.


Driven by the desire to decode this complexity, I moved to the University of Bologna to pursue a Master’s in Bioinformatics. Currently in my second year, I have transitioned from observing biological systems to modeling them.



I view myself as an In Silico Biologist. I don’t just run tools; I build and benchmark workflows to ensure biological questions are answered with statistical rigor. My current toolkit includes:

Genomics: Analyzing NGS data (FastQC, BWA, SAMtools) and exploring epigenetic landscapes via DNA methylation arrays.
Machine Learning: Implementing SVMs and Markov Models to predict protein features and functional domains.
Workflow Engineering: Utilizing Snakemake and Bash to create reproducible, scalable research pipelines.




When I’m not optimizing a Snakemake pipeline or exploring the historic porticos of Bologna, I’m usually writing about my latest technical hurdles on my blog. I believe in open science and the power of clear documentation.

title: Hello World: The In Silico Biologist
text: This is my first post on my new portfolio. I’ll be documenting my Master’s journey in Bologna here.

title: Projects Portfolio
text:
Statistical Models vs. SVM Objective: To benchmark traditional statistical methods against machine learning algorithms for the accurate prediction of signal peptides based on sequence context.
Biological Context: Signal peptides act as crucial molecular “shipping labels” that direct proteins to their correct cellular locations. Accurately predicting these cleavage sites is essential for understanding protein localization and function.
Computational Approach: In this project, we compared two distinct methodologies to evaluate predictive accuracy:

Statistical Baseline

Utilized the classical von Heijne method.
Relied on weight matrices and sequence statistics.

Machine Learning (SVM)

Developed a Support Vector Machine classifier.
Feature Engineering:

Moved beyond raw sequence strings.
Trained on context-based physiochemical features.
Included: hydrophobicity, charge, and side-chain volume.









Features were extracted from amino acid sequences using a sliding-window approach to capture local sequence context.



Tech Stack:

Python & Scikit-learn for SVM implementation and model evaluation.
Bash scripting for initial data wrangling and sequence parsing.
Custom Python scripts for extracting sliding-window physiochemical features.

Future Directions: Building upon the feature-engineered SVM, a Deep Learning implementation of this classifier is currently under active development to capture more complex, non-linear sequence patterns.




Illumina HumanMethylation450 BeadChip (450K Array)
Objective: To identify significant epigenetic signatures and methylation patterns across distinct sample groups using high-throughput array data.
Biological Context: DNA methylation is a key epigenetic mechanism that regulates gene expression without altering the DNA sequence. By analyzing these patterns, we can uncover biological markers associated with specific conditions or developmental stages.
The “In Silico” Pipeline:

Data Preprocessing & QC

Imported raw IDAT files using the minfi package in R.
Conducted quality control checks and normalized data to remove technical variation.

Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

Performed Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to visualize global methylation differences between groups.
Generated Heatmaps of the most variable CpG sites to identify clustering patterns.

Differential Methylation Analysis

Applied statistical tests to identify Differentially Methylated Positions (DMPs).
Evaluated biological significance through fold-change and p-value filtering.


Tech Stack:

R & Bioconductor (minfi, limma) for statistical analysis.
ggplot2 & pheatmap for high-quality biological visualizations.


Key Insight: Utilizing PCA allowed us to confirm that the primary source of variance in the dataset was indeed the biological group, validating the experimental design.

title: Projects Portfolio
text:
Structural Bioinformatics

Kunitz Domain Annotation via Markov Models
Detailed write-up coming soon. (Focus: Classifying functional domains in protein structure-derived alignments using Hidden Markov principles.)

title: The In Silico Biologist
text:
I am a second-year Master of Bioinformatics student at the University of Bologna, building upon my foundation in Biology from the University of Shiraz.


My work sits at the intersection of molecular biology and computational efficiency. I am currently focused on: * NGS Pipelines: Developing reproducible workflows using Snakemake. * Epigenetics: Analyzing DNA methylation patterns (450K arrays) in R. * Protein Informatics: Using Machine Learning (SVMs and Markov Models) to predict signal peptides and annotate domains.

Exploring biological complexity through the lens of computation.
